title: fix: let TD003 behave the same for urls and issue codes
body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to Ruff/ty! To help us out with reviewing, please consider the following:

- Does this pull request include a summary of the change? (See below.)
- Does this pull request include a descriptive title? (Please prefix with `[ty]` for ty pull
  requests.)
- Does this pull request include references to any relevant issues?
- Does this PR follow our AI policy (https://github.com/astral-sh/.github/blob/main/AI_POLICY.md)?
-->

## Summary

<!-- What's the purpose of the change? What does it do, and why? -->

The goal of this change is to allow the TD003 rule to behave consistently between different regexps, i.e., to capture extra text after the capture of the url / issue code is done.

Follow up of #24156 but with testing and from my personal profile.

## Test Plan

Added "accepted" test cases to prove the new regex groups and tested locally with `cargo test`
author: Crissal1995

linked_issues.title: Allow extra characters on next line for TD003

issue_comments.body: Thanks for updating the PR. Has there been any previous discussion motivating this change (issue?)? I'm asking because I think the way the regexes are written today is very intentional to avoid false positives: It's easier to detect a URL with high confidence than it is an issue code.

The issue is that currently it is possible to match `https://generic-url and some other text`, but not `TODO JIRA-1234: explanation`. This PR aims to fix it.
Also not sure why the PR was closed, is something still missing @MichaReiser ?

raw_diff:
diff --git a/crates/ruff_linter/resources/test/fixtures/flake8_todos/TD003.py b/crates/ruff_linter/resources/test/fixtures/flake8_todos/TD003.py
index 87bf5291aa0794..8dfbed29c4fff9 100644
--- a/crates/ruff_linter/resources/test/fixtures/flake8_todos/TD003.py
+++ b/crates/ruff_linter/resources/test/fixtures/flake8_todos/TD003.py
@@ -2,15 +2,29 @@
 # TODO: this comment has a link
 # https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/issues/3870
 
+# TODO: this comment has a link with some text afterwards
+# https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/issues/3870 is the url of the ticket in our internal jira
+
 # TODO: this comment has an issue
 # TDO-3870
 
+# TODO: this comment has an issue without any code prefix
+# this was already in place, but not tested?
+# 3870
+
+# TODO: this comment has an issue without any code prefix
+# and some other text after it
+# 3870 is the id of the ticket in our internal jira
+
 # TODO: the link has an issue code of the minimum length
 # T-001
 
 # TODO: the issue code can be arbitrarily long
 # ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ-001
 
+# TODO: the issue code can be followed by text
+# TD003 is the id of the ticket in our internal jira
+
 # TDO003 - errors
 # TODO: this comment has no
 # link after it
diff --git a/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/rules/todos.rs b/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/rules/todos.rs
index b46e4d44aa23f7..26c0cc81fec2ee 100644
--- a/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/rules/todos.rs
+++ b/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/rules/todos.rs
@@ -102,6 +102,12 @@ impl Violation for MissingTodoAuthor {
 ///
 /// # TODO(charlie): this comment has an issue code (matches the regex `[A-Z]+\-?\d+`)
 /// # SIXCHR-003
+/// 
+/// # TODO(charlie): this comment has a 3-digit issue code (matches the regex `\d+`)
+/// # 003 will resolve this todo comment
+/// 
+/// # TODO(charlie): this comment has an issue code (matches the regex `[A-Z]+\-?\d+`)
+/// # SIXCHR-003 will resolve this todo comment
 /// ```
 #[derive(ViolationMetadata)]
 #[violation_metadata(stable_since = "v0.0.269")]
@@ -241,8 +247,8 @@ impl Violation for MissingSpaceAfterTodoColon {
 static ISSUE_LINK_OWN_LINE_REGEX_SET: LazyLock<RegexSet> = LazyLock::new(|| {
     RegexSet::new([
         r"^#\s*(http|https)://.*", // issue link
-        r"^#\s*\d+$",              // issue code - like "003"
-        r"^#\s*[A-Z]+\-?\d+$",     // issue code - like "TD003"
+        r"^#\s*\d+.*",             // issue code - like "003"
+        r"^#\s*[A-Z]+\-?\d+.*",    // issue code - like "TD003"
     ])
     .unwrap()
 });
diff --git a/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/snapshots/ruff_linter__rules__flake8_todos__tests__missing-todo-link_TD003.py.snap b/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/snapshots/ruff_linter__rules__flake8_todos__tests__missing-todo-link_TD003.py.snap
index 6786d440b6233c..cecbfb5fa37abf 100644
--- a/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/snapshots/ruff_linter__rules__flake8_todos__tests__missing-todo-link_TD003.py.snap
+++ b/crates/ruff_linter/src/rules/flake8_todos/snapshots/ruff_linter__rules__flake8_todos__tests__missing-todo-link_TD003.py.snap
@@ -2,63 +2,63 @@

 ---

-29 | # TDO-3870

-36 |
-37 | # TODO: https://github.com/astral-sh/ruff/pull/9627 A todo with a link on the same line
+50 |

    |
 
 TD003 Missing issue link for this TODO
-  --> TD003.py:41:3

    |
-39 | # TODO: #9627 A todo with a number on the same line
-40 |

    |   ^^^^
-42 |

-  --> TD003.py:43:3

label_notes: Maintainer-explicit-design-pushback on regex-loosening change. Crissal1995 (prior_prs=0, NONE) submitted +54/-34 across 3 files loosening TD003 regexes from anchored $ to permissive .* to allow trailing text after issue codes like TD003 is the id of the ticket in our internal jira. Follow-up to abandoned #24156 ('with testing and from my personal profile' — suggests previous attempt was from work-account or different author). MichaReiser (MEMBER) raised explicit design concern: 'Has there been any previous discussion motivating this change (issue?)? I'm asking because I think the way the regexes are written today is very intentional to avoid false positives: It's easier to detect a URL with high confidence than it is an issue code.' Closed 9 days later without further author engagement at closure time. Crissal1995 commented one month after closure asking 'Also not sure why the PR was closed, is something still missing @MichaReiser?' — too late for revival, and the closure had no explicit content reason recorded, but maintainer's design-intent question stood unaddressed. The change broadens issue-code-regex (e.g. ^#\\s*\\d+$ → ^#\\s*\\d+.*) which would significantly increase false-positive surface area — any TODO followed by a numeric line would now match (e.g., a TODO followed by '5431 something' would silence the warning). Maintainer's false-positive concern is well-founded: stricter anchored regex was deliberately chosen for precision/recall tradeoff. Adds: regex-loosening-without-precision-recall-analysis rejected_quality sub-pattern + first-time-contributor-supersession-followup variant (referencing abandoned #24156). Diversity: 4th ruff rejected_quality case (charlie#22067, seroperson#24239, XuehaiPan#24572, this) — should slow on ruff after this, but methodologically this is a distinct linter-rule-design rejection vs prior 3 (formatter design, financial-incentive policy, soundness counterexample).
